nodes.name: 南里奥格兰德州
nodes.summary: 南里奥格兰德州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联89种鸟类，包括孤䳍、黄腿穴䳍、Long-trained nightjar等89种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 帕拉伊巴州

nodes.name: 美洲
nodes.summary: 美洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联24种鸟类，包括疣鼻棲鴨、Pied-billed grebe、Pale-vented pigeon等24种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Social flycatcher
nodes.summary: Social flycatcher（Social flycatcher，学名 Myiozetetes similis），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点。

nodes.name: 米西奧內斯省 (阿根廷)
nodes.summary: 米西奧內斯省 (阿根廷)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联58种鸟类，包括孤䳍、Purple-winged ground dove、Long-trained nightjar等58种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Myiozetetes | species = similis | authority = (Spix, 1825) | synonyms = Muscicapa similis (protonym) | range_map = Myiozetetes similis map.svg }} The social flycatcher (Myiozetetes similis) is a passerine bird from the Americas, a member of the large tyrant flycatcher family Tyrannidae. It is found in Mexico, every Central American country, and every mainland South American country except Chile, Guyana, Suriname, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Myiozetetes | species = similis | authority = (Spix, 1825) | synonyms = Muscicapa similis (protonym) | range_map = Myiozetetes similis map.svg }} The social flycatcher (Myiozetetes similis) is a passerine bird from the Americas, a member of the large tyrant flycatcher family Tyrannidae. It is found in Mexico, every Central American country, and every mainland South American country except Chile, Guyana, Suriname, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: pallidiventris: eastern Brazil roughly bounded by eastern Pará, Paraíba, northern Rio Grande do Sul, and Mato Grosso do Sul continuing into eastern Paraguay and northeastern Argentina's Misiones Province The species has also occurred as a vagrant in the United States with at least one record in Texas. The social flycatcher inhabits a variety of moist to semi-arid landscapes in the tropical and lower subtropical zones, most of which are somewhat open
